safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_tg (carried 06-19) breaker buy $102.50 = 3M breaker 0.5 + HTF 0.618 + $100 psych + 2W demand $99.50; matches the blue zone on the COP 3M chart (0.5 ≈ 104, 0.618 ≈ 102, $100 psych, 2W demand floor 99.68)

targets.basis: COP 3M chart VAH / 5D green line (~119–120); also the 06-05 conditional 'breaker ~120 reclaim' level

safest_plan.stop_basis: close below the 3M breaker block / 2W demand (~99.68 on the COP 3M chart); a close below that points to 3M demand 92–94 (zero_tg invalidation: 'close below the 3M BB → 3M demand $94')

stance_by_source.summary: Cleanest in-lane halal spot buy — breaker dip at $102.50 with stacked confluence; targets the 120 region, invalid below the breaker.
Accumulate ~100 ONLY after oil reclaims fair value and the 120 breaker — explicitly conditional, and that condition is not yet met; targets 135/145/150.
Underlying WTI crude (the driver for COP) is in a failed auction, rolling off ~96 toward the 84–88 demand box with a drawn path to ~80/70.
Value-at-high retest around 98–99 as a reload, invalid on a close below value-at-low.
Reaccumulate near 80 if a correction comes; long-run target the old supply high ~145+.

conflicts: Direction clash: the COP stock chart frames a bullish dip-buy at ~100–104, but the underlying WTI crude charts (newest, 06-05) show a failed auction falling from ~96 toward 84–80 with a drawn path to ~70 — that oil weakness threatens to drive COP straight through the breaker to 3M demand 92–94.

The 06-05 entry was explicitly conditional — 'accumulate ~100 only after oil reclaims fair value + the 120 breaker' — and the oil charts show that condition is NOT met, so the bullish trigger has not actually fired.
Timing: COP last printed ~107.74 (06-19), trading ABOVE the stated 100–104 buy zone, so the planned entry is not yet available; you would be chasing if you bought here.

Text vs image (minor): extracted entry '$102.50' is consistent with the chart's blue breaker zone (0.5 ≈ 104, 0.618 ≈ 102, $100 psych, 2W demand 99.68) — $102.50 is the midpoint, no correction needed.
plain_english: ConocoPhillips is the one clean, faith-compliant oil stock here, and on its own chart it has a tidy buy zone around $100–104 with room up toward $120. The catch: this stock follows the oil price, and every oil chart in the set shows oil weakening and likely heading lower — and the original plan said only buy once oil turns back up, which it hasn't. The stock is also currently above the buy zone, not in it. So don't buy now: wait for the price to actually fall into the $100–104 zone AND for oil to stop falling and start recovering before committing; if it instead closes below about $99–100, step aside and look again near $92–94.
